001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.SystemUtils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.math.*;
024import java.time.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.concurrent.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
033
034/**
035 * Parses BSON (Binary JSON) into POJO models.
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>Media types:</h5>
038 *
039 * Handles <c>Content-Type</c> types:  <bc>application/bson</bc>
040 *
041 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
042 * <p>
043 * BSON-to-Java type mapping:
044 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
045 *    <li>BSON document (0x03) → bean or {@link Map}
046 *    <li>BSON array (0x04) → {@link Collection} or Java array (numeric string keys ignored)
047 *    <li>BSON string (0x02) → {@link String}; coerced to types parseable from string
048 *    <li>BSON boolean (0x08) → {@link Boolean}
049 *    <li>BSON int32 (0x10) → {@code int}; coerced to {@code long}, {@code short}, {@code byte} as needed
050 *    <li>BSON int64 (0x12) → {@code long}; coerced to {@code int} if target is {@code int}
051 *    <li>BSON double (0x01) → {@code double}; coerced to {@code float}
052 *    <li>BSON datetime (0x09) → {@link Date}, {@link Calendar}, {@link Instant}, or
053 *       {@code long} (millis)
054 *    <li>BSON binary (0x05) → {@code byte[]}
055 *    <li>BSON decimal128 (0x13) → {@link BigDecimal}
056 *    <li>BSON null (0x0A) → {@code null}
057 *    <li>BSON ObjectId (0x07) → 24-character hex {@link String}
058 *    <li>Deprecated/unsupported types → silently skipped
059 * </ul>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
062 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
063 * </ul>
064 *
065 * <h5 class='section'>Limitations:</h5><ul>
066 *    <li>BSON requires a top-level document. Standalone scalars are wrapped as <c>{value: x}</c>.
067 *    <li>Map keys must be strings. Non-string keys use <c>toString()</c>. Null keys use a configurable placeholder.
068 *    <li>ObjectId (0x07) is read-only; parsed as 24-character hex strings.
069 *    <li>Deprecated types (Undefined, DBPointer, Symbol, JS with Scope) are silently skipped.
070 *    <li>MongoDB-internal types (JavaScript, Timestamp, MinKey, MaxKey) are not supported.
071 *    <li>Decimal128 (0x13) NaN and ±Infinity have no {@link BigDecimal} form; they are normalized to <jk>null</jk>.
072 *    <li>Wire-declared lengths (strings, binary, document/array size prefixes) are bounds-checked against a
073 *       configurable maximum (default 16 MiB, see {@link Builder#maxLength(int)}); malformed/adversarial lengths
074 *       fail with a clean {@link ParseException} instead of an unchecked error.
075 *    <li>BSON is binary; <c>SpacedHex</c> and <c>Base64</c> subclasses accept text-encoded input.
076 * </ul>
077 *
078 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
079 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Bson">BSON Basics</a>
080 * </ul>
081 */
082@SuppressWarnings({
083   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for this class hierarchy
084   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
085   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
086})
087public class BsonParser extends InputStreamParser implements BsonMetaProvider, RecordReadable, ArrayRecordReadable {
088
089   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
090   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
091   private static final String DEFAULT_NULL_KEY = "<NULL>";
092   private static final int DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH = BsonInputStream.DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH;
093
094   /** Default parser, string input encoded as BASE64. */
095   public static class Base64 extends BsonParser {
096
097      /**
098       * Constructor.
099       *
100       * @param builder The builder for this object.
101       */
102      public Base64(Builder builder) {
103         super(builder.binaryFormat(BinaryFormat.BASE64));
104      }
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Builder class.
109    */
110   public static class Builder extends InputStreamParser.Builder<Builder> {
111
112      private static final Cache<HashKey,BsonParser> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, BsonParser.class).build();
113
114      private String nullKeyString = DEFAULT_NULL_KEY;
115      private int maxLength = DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH;
116
117      /**
118       * Constructor, default settings.
119       */
120      protected Builder() {
121         consumes("application/bson");
122         nullKeyString = env("BsonParser.nullKeyString", DEFAULT_NULL_KEY);
123         maxLength = env("BsonParser.maxLength", DEFAULT_MAX_LENGTH);
124      }
125
126      /**
127       * Copy constructor.
128       *
129       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
130       */
131      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
132         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
133         nullKeyString = copyFrom.nullKeyString;
134         maxLength = copyFrom.maxLength;
135      }
136
137      /**
138       * Copy constructor.
139       *
140       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
141       */
142      protected Builder(BsonParser copyFrom) {
143         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
144         nullKeyString = copyFrom.nullKeyString;
145         maxLength = copyFrom.maxLength;
146      }
147
148      @Override /* InputStreamParser.Builder<?> */
149      public Builder binaryFormat(BinaryFormat value) {
150         super.binaryFormat(value);
151         return this;
152      }
153
154      /**
155       * The string used to represent <jk>null</jk> map keys in BSON documents.
156       *
157       * <p>
158       * BSON document field names cannot be <jk>null</jk>. When parsing a field with this name,
159       * it is stored with <jk>null</jk> as the key in the target map.
160       *
161       * @param value The placeholder string for null keys. Default is <js>"&lt;NULL&gt;"</js>.
162       * @return This object.
163       */
164      public Builder nullKeyString(String value) {
165         nullKeyString = value == null ? DEFAULT_NULL_KEY : value;
166         return this;
167      }
168
169      /**
170       * The maximum allowed wire-declared length (in bytes) for BSON strings, binary payloads, and document/array
171       * size prefixes.
172       *
173       * <p>
174       * Guards against malformed or adversarial input where a small payload declares a huge or negative length,
175       * which would otherwise trigger {@link OutOfMemoryError} or {@link NegativeArraySizeException}.  Malformed
176       * lengths are reported as a clean parse error instead.
177       *
178       * @param value The maximum length in bytes.  Default is 16 MiB (the conventional BSON document-size limit).
179       *    Values &le; 0 disable the cap (only the negative-length check remains).
180       * @return This object.
181       */
182      public Builder maxLength(int value) {
183         maxLength = value;
184         return this;
185      }
186
187      @Override /* Parser.Builder<?> */
188      public Builder trimStrings() {
189         super.trimStrings();
190         return this;
191      }
192
193      @Override /* Parser.Builder<?> */
194      public Builder trimStrings(boolean value) {
195         super.trimStrings(value);
196         return this;
197      }
198
199      @Override /* Context.Builder<?> */
200      public BsonParser build() {
201         return cache(CACHE).build(BsonParser.class);
202      }
203
204      @Override /* Context.Builder<?> */
205      public HashKey hashKey() {
206         return HashKey.of(super.hashKey(), nullKeyString, maxLength);
207      }
208
209      @Override /* Context.Builder<?> */
210      public Builder copy() {
211         return new Builder(this);
212      }
213   }
214
215   /** Default parser, string input encoded as spaced-hex. */
216   public static class SpacedHex extends BsonParser {
217
218      /**
219       * Constructor.
220       *
221       * @param builder The builder for this object.
222       */
223      public SpacedHex(Builder builder) {
224         super(builder.binaryFormat(BinaryFormat.SPACED_HEX));
225      }
226   }
227
228   /** Default parser, all default settings. */
229   public static final BsonParser DEFAULT = new BsonParser(create());
230   /** Default parser, spaced-hex string input. */
231   public static final BsonParser DEFAULT_SPACED_HEX = new SpacedHex(create());
232   /** Default parser, BASE64 string input. */
233   public static final BsonParser DEFAULT_BASE64 = new Base64(create());
234
235   /**
236    * Creates a new builder for this object.
237    *
238    * @return A new builder.
239    */
240   public static Builder create() {
241      return new Builder();
242   }
243
244   private final Map<ClassMeta<?>,BsonClassMeta> bsonClassMetas = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
245   private final Map<BeanPropertyMeta,BsonBeanPropertyMeta> bsonBeanPropertyMetas = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
246   private final String nullKeyString;
247   private final int maxLength;
248
249   /**
250    * Constructor.
251    *
252    * @param builder The builder for this object.
253    */
254   public BsonParser(Builder builder) {
255      super(builder);
256      nullKeyString = builder.nullKeyString;
257      maxLength = builder.maxLength;
258   }
259
260   @Override /* Context */
261   public Builder copy() {
262      return new Builder(this);
263   }
264
265   @Override /* Context */
266   public BsonParserSession.Builder createSession() {
267      return BsonParserSession.create(this);
268   }
269
270   @Override /* BsonMetaProvider */
271   public BsonBeanPropertyMeta getBsonBeanPropertyMeta(BeanPropertyMeta bpm) {
272      if (bpm == null)
273         return BsonBeanPropertyMeta.DEFAULT;
274      return bsonBeanPropertyMetas.computeIfAbsent(bpm, k -> new BsonBeanPropertyMeta(k.getDelegateFor(), this));
275   }
276
277   @Override /* BsonMetaProvider */
278   public BsonClassMeta getBsonClassMeta(ClassMeta<?> cm) {
279      return bsonClassMetas.computeIfAbsent(cm, k -> new BsonClassMeta(k, this));
280   }
281
282   @Override /* Context */
283   public BsonParserSession getSession() {
284      return createSession().build();
285   }
286
287   /**
288    * Returns the string used to represent <jk>null</jk> map keys in BSON documents.
289    *
290    * @return The null key placeholder string.
291    */
292   public String getNullKeyString() {
293      return nullKeyString;
294   }
295
296   /**
297    * Returns the maximum allowed wire-declared length (in bytes) for strings, binary payloads, and document/array
298    * size prefixes.
299    *
300    * @return The maximum length in bytes.
301    */
302   public int getMaxLength() {
303      return maxLength;
304   }
305
306   /**
307    * Convenience delegator for the whole-value {@link RecordReader} using <b>default session
308    * arguments</b>.  The real implementation lives on {@link BsonParserSession#parseRecords(Object)}.
309    *
310    * @param input The input.
311    * @return A new {@link RecordReader} cursor.
312    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
313    */
314   @Override /* RecordReadable */
315   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
316      return getSession().parseRecords(input);
317   }
318
319   /**
320    * Convenience delegator for the buffered array-element {@link RecordReader} using <b>default
321    * session arguments</b>.  The real implementation lives on
322    * {@link BsonParserSession#parseArrayRecords(Object)}.
323    *
324    * @param input The input.
325    * @return A buffered {@link RecordReader}.
326    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading the input.
327    */
328   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
329   public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
330      return getSession().parseArrayRecords(input);
331   }
332
333   /**
334    * The BSON record cursor is buffered/{@link RecordAdapter}-backed, not O(1) streaming.
335    *
336    * @return Always <jk>false</jk>.
337    */
338   @Override /* RecordReadable */
339   public boolean isRecordStreaming() { return false; }
340
341   /**
342    * The BSON array-record cursor is buffered/{@link RecordAdapter}-backed, not O(1) streaming.
343    *
344    * @return Always <jk>false</jk>.
345    */
346   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
347   public boolean isArrayRecordStreaming() { return false; }
348}
